Off Calendar May-19-2021 Continued To Jul-14-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

The following matters were heard: PES-16-299969 Estate of Catherine Jair and
PES-20-303882 Estate of Raymond Jair.

Appearances: Carolyn Espey appeared by zoom for Sharon So Epprecht (Objector in Catherine's estate) and for Sylvia Tan (Objector in Raymond's estate).
Sheila Robello and Vincent Yu appeared by zoom for and with Paul Perdue (Petitioner/Proposed Administrator) by zoom.
Paul Perdue appeared by zoom for Jack Jair (Former Administrator in Catherine's estate).

Ms. Espey referred to the April 16th order requiring Jack Jair to provide bank statements, tax records, etc., stated that some information was provided, but bank statements were not provided, that they found out that Mr. Jair has collected rents and is depositing the rents into property management accounts which include other properties that Jack is managing, and that Mr. Jair has served K-1's for the tax year of 2020, but they do not have a copy of the 2020 tax return. Ms. Espey also stated that overall the court order has not been complied with properly, that what has been provided cannot be connected to anything concrete, that there is about $16,000.00 a month of rental income coming in and that they have no choice other than to have a Receiver appointed. The Court stated that it will not issue an order without filing of a petition for appointment of a Receiver. Ms. Espey requested a continuance and requested that Mr. Perdue's petition for appointment be denied and taken off calendar. The Court noted that this would leave no Administrator and that it makes things worse. Mr. Yu stated that they provided over 200 pages of charts, Mr. Jair's notes, and details on rents, utilities, taxes, and pro rata shares for each Beneficiary. The Court stated that a personal fashion of maintaining records is no longer appropriate once a person becomes a fiduciary, that it is troubled that no work was done until April, questions what happened in March. and stated that it is not impressed with the progress. Mr. Perdue stated that Mr. Jair's CPA couldn't deal with this because of the tax season, that the CPA was retained in the last two years and only has records for 2019 and 2020, and that he requested copies of the tax returns from the IRS around April 20th. Mr. Perdue also stated that he is getting copies of the Bank of America checking account and savings account from Mr. Jair, that they have receipt books for the rents (about three books for each property), that he is prepared to produce everything they have, that he hopes he will get his hands on the bank statements by next week and that the bank accounts are historically where the rents have been deposited. Discussion was held as to why things were to be provided on a rolling basis. The Court stated that Mr. Perdue represents Jack Jair, that there is a significant conflict, that Paul Perdue cannot be appointed as the Administrator of these estates as the conflict runs too deep and that they need someone to step forward and manage these estates. The Court also noted that there are related cases from 1988, stated that the parties need to work together and either file a petition for appointment of a Receiver, an Administrator or something else appointed and that if there is no petition at the next calling, it will consider doing something sua sponte. The order for production remains in place. The Court expects to see that bank records are in Ms. Espey's hands within a week and that things are to be provided on a rolling basis. The Petitions for Letters of Administration in both estates are denied. Status on filing of the substitution of attorney is off calendar. The Petition for Approval of Report on Status of Administration is continued to and status on filing of a petition for appointment of a Personal Representative or a Receiver is set for July 14, 2021 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.
Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Tauscher. Not reported.

The Court noted that this matter was continued from the last hearing for filing of an amended petition and for filing of a petition for final distribution, or a petition for approval of continued administration, that it does not have an amended petition and that instead it has something titled in the form of a supplement. Mr. Perdue stated that he has provided Ms. Espey with a proposed order, that the supplemental declaration separates out the pay on death accounts, that he thought this is what was needed and that he was not clear from the last hearing that he had to amend the petition and file a report. Ms. Espey stated that there were several bank accounts that were pay on death accounts, that the bank wanted her Client and the Brother there at the same time, that it did not happen and that sometime after she got a call from the Bank that the Brother was there and that he needed something to be signed. Ms. Espey also stated that her Client thought she was signing something that would get her share of the accounts to her, that what her Client signed was a document that allowed these accounts to be transferred to Jack as the Executor and that her Client did not understand what she was signing. Mr. Perdue stated that the accounts got inventoried because he did not know at the time that they were pay on death accounts. The Court stated that with all of these mistakes that were made one would normally see this as an 850 petition, and questions whether the existing petition can be construed as an appropriate petition for what is being sought, whether it needs to be amended and if there are any other interested parties. Mr. Perdue stated that there are two Beneficiaries that are to receive specific amounts and that they will be taken care of. Ms. Espey stated that she does not believe that the relief being sought can be done by this petition and that she thinks it needs to be amended, or they need a new petition. Mr. Perdue stated that he prefers amending and having a date certain. The Petition for Preliminary Distribution is continued to October 29, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Mr. Perdue is to get his amended petition filed and served quickly. Status on close of estate is also continued to October 29, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Mr. Perdue is to have either a petition for final distribution, or a petition for approval of report on status of administration filed by October 22, 2019.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Chan. Not reported.

Petition For Preliminary Distribution
Off Calendar Aug-13-2019 Continued To Sep-17-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances: Paul Perdue for Jack Jair. Carolyn Espey for Hazel So.

Mr. Perdue stated that Mr. Jair was here at the last hearing, that there is an amount of $200,000.00 that they need to account for, that Mr. Jair has gone through his records with no luck at accounting for the $200,000.00, that there are pay-on-death accounts that were collected & are in the estate that should not be there, and that they would like the Court to sign an order allowing a release to Ms. Espey's client, et al. Mr. Perdue stated that since the accounts are listed in the Inventory that they feel that there is a need to track what is being done. The Court inquired whether there was anything before it today on this issue. Mr. Perdue stated that a Petition for Preliminary Distribution is on file or that he and Ms. Espey could stipulate. Mr. Perdue also stated they could go to the bank to obtain the deposits that were made and that they can track most of the deposits in the account. The Court stated that if Jack Jair can't get it done then someone else needs to get it done. Mr. Perdue stated that they could call First Republic Bank to find out if they have all of the deposit statements, that Jack's records are all over the place, and that Jack knows his system - he doesn't. Ms. Espey stated that her client's focus is on the pay-on-death assets, that her client is elderly, that her client has stated that she'd like to receive the pay-on-death funds before "she leaves this earth", and that she thinks that the bank will require a court order. Ms. Espey also stated that the $723,000.00 is her client's share of the pay-on-death assets minus two gifts that will need to be made. Brief discussions are held as to whether or not the current petition gets the Court there on the pay-on-death issue and whether or not a petition to approve a partial distribution needs to be filed. The Court stated that it was inclined to leave the Petition for Preliminary Distribution on calendar for Mr. Perdue to amend and that it is inclined to take the First Account and Report off calendar. Ms. Espey stated that Mr. Jair could contact each and every account from date of death forward, provide that information to her and her client, and then he could file an accounting. The Court stated that this not a good indication that Mr. Jair can perform his fiduciary duties. The Court then continued the Petition for Preliminary Distribution and Account to September 17, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status on the filing of a petition for final distribution or a new petition for continued administration. The Court took the pending First Account and Report of Administration off calendar. The Court stated that it is communicating an urgency to Mr. Jair to get this done without it pulling the suspension trigger and that if Mr. Jair's records don't get him there that's not the end of his story.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Deputy: C. Kabasares. Matter not reported.
